description: Pinned real PR-shaped diffs across languages for the review-surfaces effectiveness benchmark (BENCH.1 + BENCH.2). Each case pins a public repo + base/head commit SHAs; `node bench/run.mjs` clones the two commits on demand and runs the full `all` pipeline (mock provider, hermetic). `expected_focus` is OPTIONAL (adds the top-5 recall metric); the other metrics need no annotation. The seed leans on EXCLUSION-STRESS cases — source mixed with docs / lockfiles (go.sum, Cargo.lock, uv.lock, Package.resolved) / CI workflows / config / binary snapshots — so the floor's exclusion is exercised on real diffs. BENCH.2: the Swift/SwiftPM/iOS cases (lang "swift") span the six required shapes — a SwiftPM public-declaration change, app source + matching XCTest, a Swift Testing integration change, a package requirement/pin change, an entitlement/privacy-manifest config change, and a mixed source+tests+docs+generated-churn case — each carrying expected_focus where a single primary file is intended. Add cases freely; target 20-30.

cases.head: 6bb6c9ae9970387eeb4b8394c34bd8c350030500

cases.head: af9607f9d9ca0f6dc361337faa45f96b74888567

cases.head: 05b6f05b55ff9e2dcdcc21ac78c6ba81ea624791
cases.note: BENCH.2 shape: Swift Testing integration change (native image attachments, #1067) — single-file change to the Swift Testing surface in AssertSnapshot.swift. Distinct from the Swift Testing WEAKENING case below

cases.head: b3458b1fe9230deabd20a6f87f40205f04d3d363
cases.note: BENCH.2 shape: Swift Testing weakening. TestScoping refactor (#965) that removes the `@Test func testSnapshotFailure()` from the Swift Testing `@Suite` (folds its withKnownIssue assertion into testSnapshot). The deterministic test-weakening detector (SEMANTIC_DIFF.6) flags `removed_test_method` on SwiftTestingTests.swift and LEADS the queue with 'Test weakening: removed test method' so a reviewer confirms coverage moved and was not silently dropped — top item is the weakened test (role `test`, the reviewed subject, not a miss); the broad TestScoping churn (.github/workflows/ci.yml, Package@swift-6.0.swift, other suites) stays out of the top-5

cases.head: ad5e3190cc63dc288f28546f9c6827efc1e9d495
cases.note: BENCH.2 shape: package requirement/pin change. Bump swift-syntax to latest (#1074) — the requirement range in Package.swift and Package@swift-5.9.swift plus the resolved pin in Package.resolved (classified as a SwiftPM lock artifact). The dependency facts surface the manifest requirement first (Package.swift leads) and the resolved pin (Package.resolved: 602.0.0 → 603.0.0) as a dep fact, NOT raw noise — so `allow_top_roles: [artifact]` opts the lock out of the irrelevant-top check for THIS subject-is-the-pin case while every other case keeps strict lock exclusion. Never a fabricated blocker

cases.head: 7eb18428e283dfbbee4f32c33b8c873ed0146011
cases.note: BENCH.2 shape: entitlement/privacy-manifest config change. Add privacy manifest (#3792) — a new Source/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy (declares NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategorySystemBootTime) plus the .pbxproj reference and the Package.swift resources entry; the Apple config/privacy facts must surface the manifest, all config (no impl source), so this is a config-shaped diff, not a fabricated blocker

cases.head: 36694f3f73d272dc311fbfd271f80bf0ac457d21
cases.note: BENCH.2 shape: mixed source+tests+binary-snapshot churn (exclusion-stress). Perceptual image precision + 90% speed improvement (#628) — 14 Snapshotting/*.swift source files + a test plus three regenerated .png/binary snapshot artifacts; intentionally UNANNOTATED (broad multi-file change), the bar is that the three generated binaries never leak into the top-5 and the queue is neither empty nor a fabricated blocker
